tides.title: The Braided Chain
tides.summary: Ongoing maintenance and upgrades on the 2004 Hunter 330 at Harbortown Marina, Merritt Island.
tides.notes: Passage from Fort Pierce complete. Paperwork (USCG docs, FL registration) all done — recheck both June 2027. Nine active project waves.

waves.title: Holding Tank Replacement
waves.summary: Replace existing holding tank with custom rigid poly tank from Ronco Plastics. Porta-potty as interim solution.
waves.notes: Custom tank design in progress. Need to confirm dimensions and order. Porta-potty covers gap in the meantime.

ripples.text: Confirm final tank dimensions
Place order with Ronco Plastics
Source porta-potty as interim
Schedule installation

waves.title: Safety & Compliance
waves.summary: Flares and fire extinguishers expiring soon, placards in rough shape. Get everything current.
waves.notes: USCG docs and FL registration complete — recheck June 2027. This wave is about physical safety equipment only.

ripples.text: Replace expired or soon-expiring flares
Replace fire extinguishers
Order and install new safety placards
Recheck USCG docs and FL registration — June 2027

waves.title: Canvas & Sails
waves.summary: Bimini needs all new canvas and a welder for a quick frame repair. Sails have failing edge stitching and one small tear in the main.
waves.notes: Bimini frame weld is a small job — find a local mobile welder. Sail stitching is edge work, likely a sailmaker visit or drop-off. Main tear is small but needs addressing before it grows.

ripples.text: Source Bimini canvas replacement — measure frame first
Find mobile welder for Bimini frame repair
Get Bimini frame welded
Install new Bimini canvas
Take sails to sailmaker — edge stitching re-stitch and main tear repair

waves.title: Rigging — DIY Replacement
waves.summary: Gradually replacing sheets and halyards yourself to reduce rigger costs.
waves.notes: Do what you can independently before handing off. Track what's been replaced and what's still original.

ripples.text: Audit all sheets and halyards — note condition and age of each
Source replacement line — measure and order
Replace sheets
Replace halyards

waves.title: Rigging — Shroud Inspection
waves.summary: One shroud showing minor wear signs noticed this weekend. Get a rigger to assess.
waves.notes: Noticed this weekend — not an emergency but should not be ignored. Get professional eyes on it before sailing offshore.

ripples.text: Document location and photos of wear on shroud
Book rigger inspection
Replace shroud if rigger recommends

waves.title: Electrical & Instruments
waves.summary: Interior lights burning out with non-replaceable bulbs, tachometer intermittently failing, instrument wiring has a loose connection causing glitches.
waves.notes: Tach and instrument glitches feel connected — likely a loose connection somewhere in the instrument wiring. Investigate as a system. Interior lights may need fixture replacement rather than bulb swap if bulbs are integrated.

ripples.text: Identify interior light fixture models — determine if bulbs are replaceable or fixtures need swapping
Replace or upgrade interior light fixtures
Trace instrument wiring — find and reseat loose connection
Test tachometer after wiring fix — confirm if related
Replace tachometer if wiring fix doesn't resolve it

waves.title: Stove Repair
waves.summary: Propane stove won't stay lit for more than ~5 minutes despite full tanks.
waves.notes: Likely a thermocouple or flame sensor issue — common on marine propane stoves. Check thermocouple first before assuming regulator or solenoid.

ripples.text: Check and clean thermocouple — most likely culprit
Replace thermocouple if cleaning doesn't resolve it
Check propane regulator and solenoid if thermocouple is fine

waves.title: Deep Clean
waves.summary: Full boat detail — hull, deck, interior, bilge.
waves.notes: Good to do before the other projects so you're working in a clean environment.

ripples.text: Clean and scrub hull
Clean deck and cockpit
Deep clean interior — cabin, berths, galley, head
Clean and inspect bilge

waves.title: Aft Berth Mattress
waves.summary: Replace the aft berth mattress.
waves.notes: Hunter 330 aft berth is an irregular shape — measure carefully before ordering. Custom cut foam is usually the most practical option for odd marine shapes.

ripples.text: Make a cardboard template of the aft berth shape
Source custom foam cut or marine mattress to template
Order and install new mattress

tides.title: The Station
tides.summary: Fort Pierce townhouse condo (1,196 sq ft) — fix up and prepare for snowbird seasonal rentals, then eventually transition as part of Winter Park move.
tides.notes: Mid-range furnishing estimate ~$13,494. Room-by-room checklist and pricing spreadsheet built previously. Most fix-up work is rental prep but worthwhile regardless.

waves.title: Front Door Lock Swap
waves.summary: Swap current keypad lock to standard lock set for renters. Standard lock already on hand.
waves.notes: Keypad is current setup for personal use. Swap to standard deadbolt/handle set before any renter arrives. Lock in hand — just needs to be done.

ripples.text: Swap keypad lock to standard lock set before renter arrival
Store keypad lock safely for reinstall after rental

waves.title: Painting
waves.summary: Whole unit needs painting. Partially done. Room list TBD — add rooms as you work through it.
waves.notes: Purely cosmetic, no wall repairs identified yet. Add specific rooms and colors to ripples as the list comes together.

ripples.text: Compile full room-by-room paint list with colors
Source paint and supplies
Complete remaining rooms

waves.title: Floor Relay
waves.summary: Floating floors were poorly installed and are gapping. Need to be relaid properly. Back burner — budget pending.
waves.notes: Purely aesthetic issue, not a safety concern. Not a priority until budget allows. Quarter round trim is a separate wave to address the gap cosmetically in the meantime.

ripples.text: Get quote for floor relay
Schedule and complete floor relay when budget allows

waves.title: Quarter Round Trim
waves.summary: Install quarter round along baseboards throughout to cover gaps from floating floor installation.
waves.notes: DIY job. Gaps are between baseboard and floor — quarter round is the right fix. Do this before floor relay becomes a priority since it's a quick cosmetic win.

ripples.text: Measure total linear footage needed
Source and buy quarter round — match floor color
Install quarter round throughout
Caulk and touch up paint along trim

waves.title: Faucets & Shower Heads
waves.summary: Descale all faucets and shower heads throughout — follow-up from previous brown water issue.
waves.notes: Brown water issue previously resolved. This is cleanup of residual buildup. DIY descale job — white vinegar soak method works well for most fixtures.

ripples.text: Descale kitchen faucet
Descale bathroom faucets
Descale shower heads

waves.title: Furnishing for Rental
waves.summary: Room-by-room furnishing to prepare for snowbird seasonal rentals. Mid-range budget ~$13,494.
waves.notes: Full checklist and pricing spreadsheet built. Work through room by room.

ripples.text: Complete living room furnishing
Complete bedroom furnishing
Complete kitchen essentials
Research rental listing platforms
Set pricing for snowbird season

waves.title: Winter Park Relocation
waves.summary: Planning relocation from Fort Pierce to Winter Park area.
waves.notes: Explored community and friendship options in Winter Park. Mortgage scenarios modeled previously.

ripples.text: Finalize timeline for move
Revisit mortgage payment scenarios

tides.notes: 52-plant catalog built. 1x10ft west-facing window bed planting plan finalized. Fruit tree options explored. Cat safety is a constraint on all plant decisions.

waves.summary: 1x10ft west-facing window bed. Planting plan finalized.
waves.notes: Plan complete. Next step is purchasing and planting.

ripples.text: Purchase plants per finalized plan
Plant and establish window bed

waves.title: Fruit Trees
waves.summary: Exploring fruit tree options for the lawn. Zone 9b compatible selections.
waves.notes: Cat safety constraint applies. Research done, no decisions made yet.

ripples.text: Finalize fruit tree selections
Determine planting locations in yard
Purchase and plant trees

waves.title: Plant Catalog
waves.summary: 52-plant catalog built in Google Sheets and HTML. Ongoing maintenance as plants are added.
waves.notes: Combined HTML file with plant catalog and drag-and-drop garden map built.

ripples.text: Keep catalog updated as new plants are added

tides.title: Duck Duck Jeep
tides.summary: Getting the Wrangler roadworthy and loved again. Currently Camden's vehicle, needs attention across safety, electrical, and cosmetics.
tides.notes: Rained in for a while before the roof was fixed 5 years ago — moisture likely root cause of several electrical issues. Soft rear window is yellowed/hazed, not cracked. Mostly sits but seatbelt issue has made roadworthiness urgent.

waves.title: Safety First
waves.summary: Urgent roadworthiness items — seatbelts, check engine light, and the catalytic converter rattle.
waves.notes: Driver's seatbelt stopped retracting this weekend — urgent. Passenger seatbelt locked up for a while but manageable. Doing both at once makes sense. Dad suspects catalytic converter casing crack is the rattle on acceleration.

ripples.text: Replace driver's side seatbelt retractor
Replace passenger seatbelt while at it
Pull check engine codes — diagnose before assuming
Inspect catalytic converter casing for crack
Repair or replace catalytic converter as needed
Oil change

waves.title: Electrical Gremlins
waves.summary: Windows finicky, stereo broken, AC broken — likely all related to old rain damage. Diagnose as a system before throwing parts at individual symptoms.
waves.notes: Jeep was rained in before the roof was fixed ~5 years ago. Worth checking fuse box and common ground points for corrosion before assuming individual component failures.

ripples.text: Inspect fuse box for corrosion or blown fuses
Check common ground points for moisture damage
Diagnose window motors/switches — test each independently
Diagnose stereo — determine if head unit or wiring
Diagnose AC — check refrigerant level first
Repair or replace components based on diagnosis

waves.title: Body & Interior
waves.summary: Rust panels, seats, rear window restoration, and a proper deep clean.
waves.notes: Rear soft window is yellowed and hazed — try plastic polish/restoration kit before replacing. Rust panels need assessment on severity before deciding DIY vs shop. Seats are aesthetic/comfort — lowest urgency.

ripples.text: Deep clean interior — establish a fresh baseline
Try plastic restoration kit on rear soft window
Replace rear soft window if restoration doesn't work
Assess rust panels — surface vs structural
Treat or replace rusted panels
Assess seats — repair, recover, or replace

tides.title: Social Goose
tides.summary: Things for and with others — friends, family, Camden, community.
tides.notes: Includes Bahamas trip planning, community building in Winter Park, and relationship work.

waves.notes: Interactive flight search dashboard built. Need to finalize flights and logistics.

tides.notes: Health tracking moved out — will find a separate home for it. This tide is Spanish, writing, Android dev, and the Tides app itself.

waves.notes: Goal is completion, not perfection. Work backwards: what is the last true thing this story says?

Work backwards from ending — what does the story finally say?
